name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 36
units: metres, typed site-investigation log (Wimpey Geotech, BS 5930 style), transcribed verbatim

intervals.verbatim: TOPSOIL
Brown clayey silty fine to coarse SAND and fine to coarse subangular and subrounded GRAVEL with many fragments of weathered sandstone and coal
Firm mottled brown and grey silty CLAY with some pockets of light brown medium to coarse sand
Medium dense light brown becoming clayey silty fine to coarse becoming fine to medium SAND with some fine to medium subrounded gravel and occasional black coal dust

Firm becoming stiff dark greyish brown sandy to very sandy very silty CLAY.  Becoming dark greyish brown very clayey very silty fine SAND from 4.50m to 5.30m, 5.75m to 6.00m, 7.30m to 8.00m, with some fine to medium subrounded occasionally subangular gravel and occasional thin bands of light brown silty fine sand (Glacial Till)
annotations: water: water-depth column reads DRY at 10.50m (21/4)
cable percussion boring to 11.15m; chiselling 0.90m-1.30m (1h), 4.70m-4.80m (½h), 9.30m-10.00m (2h) per Remarks sheet 5

intervals.verbatim: Dense light yellowish brown fine to coarse SAND and fine to coarse subangular and subrounded GRAVEL sized fragments of weathered sandstone
annotations: water: water-depth column reads DRY at 11.15m
chiselled from 11.00m with no progress (3h) per Remarks; cable percussion ends 11.15m, rotary core drilling below

intervals.verbatim: Pale orange medium occasionally fine grained highly discoloured moderately weathered thinly bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong locally moderately weak to moderately strong.  Very closely and closely spaced subhorizontal to 10° weathered bedding fractures and subvertical fractures with common thin bands of disintegrated rock.  Much black ironstaining on fractures and some coal dust.  Dark brown highly weathered and thinly laminated from 13.85m to 14.05m
modifiers: strong locally moderately weak to moderately strong
annotations: water: GWe ground-water encountered at 15.90m (also Remarks sheet 5)

intervals.verbatim: Yellow fine becoming fine to medium grained highly discoloured slightly weathered thinly bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong.  Closely occasionally very closely spaced subhorizontal commonly weathered bedding fractures.  Occasional subvertical fractures.  Some thin laminae of coal dust from between 17.40m to 18.10m
annotations: water: GWe ground-water encountered at 18.90m (also Remarks sheet 5)

intervals.verbatim: Dark and pale orange fine grained highly discoloured moderately and slightly weathered thinly bedded sometimes faintly laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong, occasionally moderately strong.  Closely spaced subhorizontal to 15° commonly clayey weathered bedding fractures.  Many thin laminae and lenses of coal dust
Yellow medium grained moderately discoloured slightly weathered thinly bedded SANDSTONE - strong to very strong.  Closely spaced 5° to 10° bedding fractures and occasional minor 70° to 80° fractures

Orange fine grained highly becoming moderately weathered thinly planar laminated silty SANDSTONE - moderately weak becoming moderately strong.  Extremely closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures.  Completely weathered from 24.50m to 24.60m

Orange and yellow medium grained slightly and slightly to moderately weathered bedded SANDSTONE - strong to very strong.  Locally moderately weak at base.  Closely and close to medium spaced subhorizontal to 10° bedding fractures.  Common thin lenses and laminae of coal dust
Black highly fractured COAL - very weak
annotations: unnamed coal; no seam correlation given on log

intervals.verbatim: Yellow and orange coarse becoming fine to medium grained moderately, locally highly, weathered bedded SANDSTONE - moderately strong and strong, locally moderately weak.  Abundant laminae, lenses and gravel sized fragments of weathered coal.  Thin weak yellow mudstone band at 29.00m.  Closely and very closely spaced 5° to 10° weathered bedding fractures.  Locally highly fractured
modifiers: moderately strong and strong, locally moderately weak
annotations: thin weak yellow mudstone band at 29.00m (within interval, not separately logged)

intervals.verbatim: Yellowish white fine and medium grained slightly and slightly to moderately weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong.  Closely and very closely spaced 10° to 20° bedding fractures and occasional 70° fractures.  Very highly fractured from 30.20m to 30.50m
annotations: interval starts 29.30m on sheet 3 ('See next sheet'); description carried on sheet 4

intervals.verbatim: Orange discoloured becoming pale grey fine grained slightly weathered thinly bedded SANDSTONE - strong to very strong.  Closely becoming very closely spaced 5° to 10° bedding fractures.  Common localised subvertical fractures.  Occasional very thin highly fractured bands
Black fresh highly fractured COAL - very weak.  Powdered coal from 33.10m to 33.25m
annotations: unnamed coal; 0.95m thick, no seam correlation given on log and no pencil marginalia

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained highly and moderately to highly weathered silty MUDSTONE - weak and very weak.  Highly discoloured orange from 33.25m to 34.05m.  Extremely closely spaced fractures in all orientations

Pale grey slightly weathered faintly ripple laminated silty SANDSTONE - strong.  Closely spaced subhorizontal to 5° bedding fractures.  Some highly fractured bands with subvertical weathered fractures and moderately strong rock fragments.  Some silty clay in fractures

Grey fine grained slightly weathered thinly planar laminated sandy micaceous SILTSTONE - moderately strong becoming strong.  Extremely closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures and common subvertical weathered fractures.  Occasional thin highly fractured bands with weak rock fragments

Pale grey slightly discoloured brown slightly weathered silty micaceous SANDSTONE - strong.  Very closely and extremely closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures and common subvertical fractures.  Locally highly fractured.  Dark grey moderately to highly weathered moderately weak and moderately strong from 39.70m to 40.50m.  Completely weathered from 40.50m to 40.70m

notes: Typed Wimpey Geotech site-investigation log, Record of Borehole 36, sheets 1-5 of 5, A167 Durham Western Bypass, Fig. 40. Boring: cable percussion to 11.15m (Dando 150), rotary core drilling to 41.00m (Dando 250 lorry mounted, 412 core barrel, air flush). Location box 5699.4E 2598.9N matches header grid 425699,542600. Ground level 81.75 m OD; commenced 20/4/90; originator MRW/AMcL 23/4/90-8/5/90, checked & approved AMcL 22/6/90. All Reduced Level (OD) values cross-check exactly against depth below ground (81.75 - depth), including final 40.75 at 41.00m; no clerk arithmetic slips. Intermediate depth marks at 12.90, 28.00, 33.50, 35.70, 38.70 are core-run/If-zone boundaries, not strata boundaries (no OD value printed), and were not transcribed as intervals. Two coals, both unnamed: 27.65-27.80m (0.15m) and 32.30-33.25m (0.95m, powdered 33.10-33.25m); no seam names in text and no pencil marginalia anywhere on the scan. Remarks (sheet 5): chiselling 0.90-1.30m (1h), 4.70-4.80m (½h), 9.30-10.00m (2h), from 11.00m no progress (3h); water added to assist boring 0.60-3.20m and may have masked occurrence of ground-water; ground-water encountered at 15.90m and 18.90m; full return of circulation air throughout rotary borehole; backfilled with bentonite/cement grout. Sheet 5 carries only the 41.00m closure, END OF BOREHOLE, and Remarks.
